channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: why does this keep happening — 171 replies — 461.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
why does this keep happening

Open thread • Replies: 171 • Updated: 2026-05-28 11:27 UTC

OP

>Link pumps to $10
>BTC instantly takes a nosedive

Why is Link not allowed to go above $10?

Early replies (first 3)

62263473
because fuck you, stinkie
it really all boils down to that
the entire crypto market exists JUST TO FUCK YOU STINKIES OVER
how does that make you feel
it makes me feel pic related

62263519
>>62263473
hey it's the dick guy

62263588
But Sirgay said they've decoupled from crypto prices

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62271116
>>62271020
>And they don't give a shit about 4chan posters
you clearly do, which is why you are here calling people "schizos" for noticing BTC dumps whenever LINK gets positive news (pic rel). i dont spend a single minute discussing people in xrp threads or twitter: why would you post 26 times in this thread?

you have done this before havent you

62271138
>>62271104
>he didn't respond to my post so i'll give him another (you)
thanks cuck

>>62271106
>wordy and spammy
There's many posters and I'm trying to get each of you in the time window I have. It's slower to condense my thoughts.

>and what does this crystal do, desperate for pussy voice

>>62271116
>you clearly do
because im here all the time should i fren post you so you know
>26 times in this thread
you all keep responding and i'm conversing back
>you have done this before havent you
yeah i really like link and want good arguments for it

I'll just say this: I'm mainly hitting you all with points I could potentially see people using on your arguments here. I'm saving your incelsheets to regurgitate back if I feel like playing on your side next time and defend the idea. This back-and-forth helps me really hone in on solid points. If you actually said anything that made me genuinely question the prosecution side, I would use that the next time I defend until some new anon said another better point, and then i'd repeat the loop. In the end I'll have the best higher tier saying that is concise yet profound. You see this a lot with oldfag posts that say very little but a lot at the same time.

62271155
>>62271138
You got SCHOLED, kid.
You didn't respond because you got buckbroken from multiple angles.
You said:
>Why none from the 2017 era?
And got shown one from the 2017 era here >>62271074
You said:
>Why none when link was in the late 22 early 23 accumulation range
And got shown a lot from 2022-2023 here >>62271096
You said:
>or even the most recent link news?
And got shown one from 2025 here >>62271116

62271158
>>62271066
>>62271155
>or even the most recent link news

62271176
>>62271155
These all got posted AFTER i said "why none".
It does refute my point of all the screenshots being from the same era, but it doesn't disprove the point that it's bullshit victimizing. Also, your tone is triggered faggot. Tell me how I know our little chat is going to emotionally bother you for hours after our talk.

>>62271116
>>62271158
My response to these are going to be the same as it's been for the others. So what I'll do is watch and create the same format when this isn't true about link when news drops, and I believe we'll find many more examples of it not happening that otherwise would get ignored in favor of the emotionally charging dumps screenshot fest here. Sound fair?

62271202
When it does 10x-20x it will be quick

62271203
>>62271202
>Motley Fool
kek baggie you've been saying this for the past 6 years, enjoy reading your boomer rag

62271402
>>62271176
Now say that without crying.

62273954
>>62271176
Notice how every single "argument" you put forth was simply swept away with a relevant example of Link suppression.
Yet here you are, still trying to drown out reality with your wordswordswords.

You're going through the mother of all cognitive dissonances.

62274025
>>62269848
>A spiritual war between good and evil

Please shut the fuck up. You are gay. Muuh spirituals.

62277201
>>62273954
You're theories only make sense to 4chan posters with a persecution kink. Bring them outside this site and you're into heavy schizo territory.

62277225
>>62277201
Fallacy ad populum, faggot. You argue like a woman

62277275
Its weird checking in on this board like once a week and seeing the usual paid fud just never stops, and how much of it just never gets responded to or probably even seen
There's at least like 10-20k hours worth of collective fuddie manpower that would have made no difference if it had never existed at all. It would be tragic if the time of third world subhumans was worth anything to begin with

62277295
>>62277225
>the stuff we say is true because we can bias it into reality y...you're just wrong for disagreeing with our spam
Half this thread is top of the debate pyramid calling me retarded every other post. You faggots wonder why ironic shit posting is all that happens here.

62277481
>>62271176
You are Jewish as fuck.

62277668
>>62263230
Imagine telling people in 2019 that in 2026 people would be theorizing that LINK is being suppressed under $10 KEK

It was supposed to be $1000 by now

62277674
>>62271202
Bearish article paid for by link whales hunting for exit liquidity

62277803
>>62277295
>30pbtid

62278136
>>62277295
You tried to double down despite another anon posting like a dozen screencaps proving that link is specifically targeted by suppression, which is just a fraction of how many times it has actually happened. Link absolutely 100 percent is a suppressed asset
So yes. You are a retard. A useless, shit-eating brown retard who sucks at his "job" trying to gaslight people on a dead board.

62278171
you link fags have ruined this entire board with your schitzo nonsense. this is proof to me that having higher-than-average iq doesnt necessarily mean that you are 'smart' if you cant understand the greater context of why your coin is failing in the first place

62278428
>>62278171
>schitzo nonsense
This thread is full of objective, verifiable proof. It cannot be denied.
At the most you can claim that this is not exceptional and the same happens to all alts, but so far nobody has ever shown a shred of proof after years of making exactly this claim.

It's schizo to deny this is happening.

62279583
>>62277275
>fud
why do cuckolds use this word? Just check the chart

62279597
Looks like LINK suppression finally killed BTC

I wonder whats gonna happen next

62279623
>>62279597
mhh....your cuckold token crashes to $2 and misses another bullrun? Just a guess

62280508
>>62277275
Remember the Windows VR cooking spirit advertising with a VR world full of corpses?
https://www.breitbart.com/tech/2020/04/14/microsoft-publishes-then-takes-down-commercial-with-spirit-cooker-marina-abramovic/

Windows served as an intermediary in spiritual attacks during my sleep and I had to switch to linux to stop them.

I am starting to think that Chainlink becoming integrated into the financial world into every transaction would conquer darkness and insert light into every transaction.

>>62271066
How does it feel to serve evil and to deny light and god?
The result is always the same because darkness cannot exist without light as they are energy vampires.
So yes this planet can be entirely corrupted and light can leave it and everything will be saved that way but even you won't enjoy that experience because you will be their next sacrifice when they run out of babies to torture.

62280516
>>62264135
>buy into your secret club we are massively advertising
kek

62282935
>>62263230
>Why is Link not allowed to go above $10?
Too many people are holding the bags from years ago desperately waiting to dump. I call it the bag pressure.
Wikipedia will be listing bag holders as the death cause of Link in a few years

62282960
If your crypto isn't useful for buying drugs you're holding a shitcoin.
Seethe.

62283111
Crypto crashed again today, while the stock market had another green day at ATH.

If Bitcoin hadn't been used to dump on Link at every turn, it would have kept up with the stock market at the very least.

62283386
>>62269753
It’s gonna be a lot less than 10k to make it. It’s probably gonna be 500. There’s only 25,000 people with more than 1k link. I’m sure 90% with 10k over are 4chan. 4chan is an aberration. 5,000 or so mentally retards who found it long before anyone else.

The make it stack right now is probably very similar to what someone finding out about ETH at $10 could reasonably put in. 10k? 20k?

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 108 replies — 247.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 108 • Updated: 2026-05-28 11:23 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62275467
>>62271712
>make gains elsewhere
>rotate into BTC
That's how I have to go about it being such a latecomer.

62275501
>>62271712
returns are too low at this point, and it can go to 0 at any time. why are you holding bags?

62276869
>>62272002
BTC doesn’t need to become the global reserve currency overnight to justify current valuations. You people still think adoption is binary for some reason.

62276880
>>62272017
CLARITY isn't going to happen, and the Strategic Bitcoin Reserve won't mean shit unless it is passed as a bill in Congress (which also won't happen)

62276888
Fear
I actually think crypto seems retarded. I also don't understand what makes Bitcoin any different than the 10,000 other shitcoins out there. Yeah Bitcoin was first, but that is the only think I can understand that makes it special compared to numerous other coins.

I feel extreme regret for not buying it. I just make myself feel better by telling myself I would have sold it when I hit my first 5x and I never would have been able to hodl this long.

I literally almost bought $100 of Bitcoin when it was like $5 each. I closed the window at the last moment because I thought it was stupid. It feels like throwing away a winning lottery card whenever I think about it.

62278410
>>62276888
You just didnt make money on crypto

62278419
>>62276880
Retard. Stop smoking

62278837
>>62274703
How you can say it's legit?

62278840
>>62275501
can go to zero? In what way

62278849
>>62274705
Delusional joker

62278858
>>62273985
It had always been fishy.

62280044
>>62278837
You can literally verify everything yourself. We’re way past the point where major players like Aave, Ledger, GoMining, a16z and others would touch obvious garbage for no reason.

62280052
>>62272017
A lot of people still view the market through old cycle assumptions.

62280441
>>62271712
Bitcoin is a liquidity sink and will be rugged in a bug deflatinary swoop when the time is right.

62280858
>>62273653
problem with index funds is you just never make enough to make it unless you're like 20-30 years at it

62282426
>>62280858
cryoto is better than everything

62282600
>>62276888
you would've sold them at $10

62282845
>>62271712
I just inherited almost a million usd and I'd like to put 100,000 into bitcoin but I don't know how :(

I'm also very concerned about getting ripped off :(

I also don't want to have to deal with taxes :(

Will you help me? Is there a way to buy it and somehow store it offline or is it only accessible online? Do I have to make some account at Binance or some other site?

62282847
>>62271712

62282849
>>62280044
There are many similar offerings ended up nothing so it's scary

62282854
>>62282845
Send it to me

62282864
>>62282845
kys
see
>>62282847

62282885
>>62282864
I don't wanna kill myself though. I just became a millionaire! Life is finally getting good for me :)

I just wanna buy a bitcoin. Why won't you help me?

62283041
>>62272050
Hmmm.. what do you mean?

62283050
>>62282849
That’s why people should wait for the actual TBV launch first instead of screaming revolution or scam this early.
At least the Aave v4 governance process is already moving now.

62283055
>>62272643
At least check it sometimes bro. You might already be retired and just don’t know it yet.

62283065
>>62272208
A 2x or 3x from here would still outperform most traditional investments by a huge margin.

62283375
>>62283050
Defi hacks are no joke

62283377
>>62282845
Why are you flexing your imagination?

62283380
>>62271712
Money

item.title: is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf — 104 replies — 190.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf

Open thread • Replies: 104 • Updated: 2026-05-28 11:17 UTC

OP

is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf

Early replies (first 3)

62275893
>>62275877
Checked and ikd but it's telling how Chris/fishy/juels et al are ignoring CLPR like their lifes depend on it.

62275918
>>62275893
>CLPR
Is this the new talking point? A acronym we have never heard of is another chainlink killer?

62275930
>>62275918
yes its very 'organically' being spammed across this board right now in every thread, not being shilled directly because they know that doesn't work here (they tried one thread and got no traction). hedera shills are the least organic in the space, likely because they're mostly bought/paid for, another anon saved screen caps of their payout program for the shills. also the leemon face memes are awful, just being ugly is not enough to create a meme you fucking jeets.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62278702
>>62275877
chainlink has more features than tech now. use the chainlink standard to lower enterprise risk

62278704
>>62278690
>it's still a bridge like any other
https://hashgraph.com/blog/clpr-a-new-bridgeless-standard-for-cross-ledger-communication/
>For this reason, Hashgraph developed CLPR (pronounced “Clipper”), a new BRIDGELESS Cross-Ledger Protocol that takes a fundamentally different approach. Rather than pooling locked assets or delegating trust to a set of permissioned validators, CLPR uses cryptographic state proofs to let tokens, data, and arbitrary messages move between ledgers directly, with no custodian, no pooled liquidity, and no single point of failure.
How CLPR works:
>In Step 1, each ledger runs CLPR endpoints: dedicated servers that communicate directly with their counterparts on the other ledger. These endpoints exchange proofs to verify and settle cross-ledger transfers of messages and tokens. At no point does any intermediary hold or control assets. If an endpoint goes offline, transfers pause and resume automatically when connectivity is restored. Funds are never at risk.
>In Step 2, once both ledgers have implemented CLPR natively, the validators themselves become the endpoints. No separate infrastructure is required. Each ledger verifies the other’s state using its own native proof mechanism, with no additional layer in between.

62278707
How many chainlink killers are we at right now?

62278710
>>62278707
This is not a Chainlink killer, it's just a CCIP killer.

62278726
>>62278704
this doesn't have the security standards of chainlink, it could be better than layerzero.

62278731
>>62278726
>cryptographic state proofs
the setup on both chains is difficult when using proofs
>CLPR’s initial deployment is interoperability between HashSphere private enterprise networks and the Hedera public network, covering both Sphere-to-Sphere and Sphere-to-Hedera transfers.
they have this integrated on their own chains, but don't have it on other blockchains that run in different environments

62278842
another day, another reason that LINK SUPREMACY is inevitable

62278958
>>62278704
>"WE DON'T USE BRIDGES OR ORACLES TO PUT EXTERNAL DATA ON CHAIN"
>uses "dedicated servers" (step 1) or "the validators themselves" (step 2) to put external data on chain
In other words it uses oracles to put external data on chain.

Also in step 2 other chains have to write CLPR into their node code.
Good luck with that.

62280692
>>62278958
>In other words it uses oracles to put external data on chain.
They never claim not to use oracles onchain, but they don't use oracles offchain so there's no bridge. It's bridgless unlike CCIP there's no third party needed.

62280745
>>62280692
>there's no third party needed
In step 1 the "dedicated servers" are third parties.
In step 2 the chain validators are supposed to moonlight as oracles. Which is retarded in itself, but that's not the worst part; because step 2 implies fundamentally changing how chain miners/validators/nodes/... work to include CLPR functionality.

62280757
>>62280745
>In step 1 the "dedicated servers" are third parties.
No they aren't. Because they are ran in each ledger. There are then only two parties. One in one ledger and the other in the other ledger. So only two parties. No third party middle-man bridge needed like CCIP.

62280772
>>62280757
CLPR:
>sender
>server on Chain A
>server on Chain B
>receiver

CCIP:
>sender
>CCIP
>receiver

hmmmmmmmm

62280805
>>62280772
CLPR:
>source ledger
>destination ledger

CCIP:
>source ledger
>router
>committing DON
>executing DON
>router
>destination ledger

62280841
>>62280805
The router is a contract you dumbass. Not a "party". CLPR uses smart contracts too.

And without fundamentally changing every chain in existence to include CLPR in their base node code, the comparison boils down to this:

CCIP:
>source ledger
>committing DON
>executing DON
>destination ledger

CLPR:
>source ledger
>dedicated server on the source ledger
>dedicated server on the destination ledger
>destination ledger

62280984
>>62280841
Right so we can argue semantics all day but the thing is that with CLPR you don't need an offchain bridge, the ledgers talk directly with each other, that's the point. This is interesting because with CCIP you have to pay a fee to Sergey each time you use it but with CLPR you don't really have to pay anyone. Users will most likely use the cheaper option. And unlike all the bridge hacks there's nothing to hack here because it's not a bridge.

62281003
>>62280984
>the ledgers talk directly with each other
Only if those leddgers fundamentally changed their source codes to include CLPR, and the ledger's miners/nodes/validators/... pull double duty as oracles too.

In the absence of that, the ledgers talk to each other via intermediaries called "dedicated servers".

62281021
>>62281003
>the ledgers talk to each other via intermediaries called "dedicated servers".
The CLPR endpoints are not intermediares because they are "inside" each ledger so to speak. It's not a bridge. It's never offchain.
>These endpoints exchange proofs to verify and settle cross-ledger transfers of messages and tokens. At no point does any intermediary hold or control assets. If an endpoint goes offline, transfers pause and resume automatically when connectivity is restored. Funds are never at risk.

62281047
>>62281021
>The CLPR endpoints are not intermediares because they are "inside" each ledger so to speak.
No they're not.
That would only happen when "once both ledgers have implemented CLPR natively", and "the validators themselves become the endpoints".
i.e. after the ledgers in question fundamentally changed their source codes to include CLPR, and the ledger's miners/nodes/validators/... pull double duty as oracles too.

62281161
>>62281047
Btw why are you so sure that CLPR will not be adopted at the validator level? Honestly seems like the ideal solution and far better than CCIP or any other bridge. Is it that you just think devs will be too lazy to change shit?

62281185
>>62281161
>why are you so sure that CLPR will not be adopted at the validator level?
The fact that every chain has to alter their base code to include CLPR functionality.

62281195
>>62281185
Yes. There is effort to be done. There is also effort to be done when a chain pays CLL to integrate CCIP. Why are those different? It's all just paying with either time or money to integrate with either CCIP or CLPR.

62281245
>>62281195
No chain is going to fundamentally change how they work for Hedera.
And Chainlink also works with cryptographic proofs end to end. Zero need to make L1 miners/validators/nodes/... pull double duty as oracles.

One of the oldest Chainlink fuds was "Ethereum will have native oracles". This CLPR shit is an even more retarded version of that.

62281268
>>62281245
This is not a Hedera thing.
Ethereum can talk with Solana with CLPR.

62281635
>>62275877
Not another LINK killer. It's over for like the 30th time

62281760
>>62275877
all the blocking they are doing shows how scared they are to lose retail funding. we need this token to crash to zero so the last 300m coins they have are worthless. this will light a fire under sergey azz. bankrupt them faster. force layoffs.

62283173
>>62283147
your pic doesn't matter because your cuckold ponzi scheme is trading at $8. Lol

62283180
>>62283173
you know what doesn't matter? all the shitcoins listed in that pic.
except for LINK of course

seethe

62283187
>>62283180
great for ChainlinkLabs, meaningless for cuckold baggies. Price + chart

62283372
>>62281268
>CLPR is not a Hedera thing
"CLPR (“clipper”) is a new bridgeless Cross-Ledger Protocol developed by Hashgraph"
https://hashgraph.com/clpr/

>>62283173
>fundamentals don't matter
So go to the casino and stop shitting up this board then.

item.title: I won about 8 LTC from a crypto casino — 29 replies — 116.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I won about 8 LTC from a crypto casino

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-05-28 11:38 UTC

OP

I have 15 in total now.. I am gonna hodl them.

Early replies (first 3)

62270209
>>62270028
Swap for XMR and hodl

62270220
>>62270209
Thats my plan but Monero's price is just too high right now. So, I will just wait and see for now. LTC is near its this years low, so it makes sense holding it or even buying more of it.

62271797
aim for 50 at least, this coin is going to power the decentralized AI agent economy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62271814
sooooo who is going to tell him?

62272140
>>62270028
I have 800

62272349
no one mentions MWEB which is one solid reason to be bullish on LTC

62272353
>>62272349
qrd?
Also what is the make it and sui stack for this?

62272539
>>62272353
It's a lightweight privacy extension, essentially. It hides the amount and receiver. It doesn't hide the sender though (though there are some tools like Hector Chu's coinjoin that would essentially do this if people used it.) Make it stack on LTC has always been 84. Same as having 21 BTC since it has a maximum supply of 84 million.

As for SUI, be very careful of VC coins people shill. Lots of insiders staking the premine waiting to dump it. I'm not saying don't buy it, just be aware of that. There will come a day when people that got it for free (including people marketing it, which is probably how you heard about it someone marketing it getting paid in the coin and not disclosing they got a shitload of them for free). Not saying they can't pump, but they can dump very hard and never recover. Fun to swing trade though.

62272587
>>62272539
How many LTC or XMR do you have sensei?

62272618
>>62272587
I stopped buying XMR when it went to $300. It could potentially be the most valuable coin, but it has so many liquidity channels cut off I'm happy with where I'm at. You should probably treat the make it stack as 21.

Both the qubic event in XMR and inflation bug in LTC (even though it was caught and didn't do permanent damage) have made me weary on privacy coins. I think you should have them, but BTC should be your core stack if you're new. If I were just entering the market, I'd probably pick up an LTC stack first since it's so cheap right now, then an XMR stack. Not necessarily a make it stack, but just something so you don't FOMO if they pump. Since BTC.d is so high, it'd pick up the alts for maybe a year (just so you don't fomo them later) and then never buy them again and focus on accumulating BTC at any price. I might be wrong and BTC just accelerates higher, but I don't think it'll go so high so fast it'll make much of a difference if you pick up the alts first. 1st generation PoW is pretty ignored right now except BTC, so they are probably safe bets. Of course I'll keep my on stack amounts a secret. Nothing good can come from telling anyone.

62272639
>>62272618
Thanks, I appreciate the advice :)

62272654
>>62272618
do you think xmr could realistically do a huge X from where it is?

62272668
>>62272654
Yeah. BTC ran to 20k in 2017 on retail fomo essentially. There has been a huge increase in the global money supply since then. Honestly, BTC could go to like 700k and it wouldn't surprise me, XMR could go to 4k and it wouldn't surprise me and LTC could go to $800 and it wouldn't surprise me. All this shit is undervalued. Another thing is fiat always dies. All fiat currencies have died historically and the governments of the world are doing the same thing that has killed all fiat currencies historically. It's just a matter of when. In the mean time though, we still have to live in a world where we settle in fiat, so don't invest more than you can afford to lose. Just treat these things as extra savings accounts.

62272838
>>62271797
BULLSHIT

62272845
>>62272539
No one said a fucking thing about sui retarded nigger he asked what a suicide stack was.

62272863
>>62271814
Who cares? Swap vs keep. It’s still a lose-lose bet in all altcoins atp, even Ai agrees lmao

62272971
>>62272845
It specifically says sui you dumb nigger. Not that guys fault you guys speak in nigger babble slang rather than typing it out.

62273235
>>62272863
Yeah Bitcoin is the only way to not loose money, why even have a bank account just keep all your life savings in bitcoin, since it's so stable and basically a guaranteed profite. You go first anon.

62273441
>>62270220
>Monero's price is just too high right now
Sub 300 is gone forever

62273491
>>62272971
you must be over 18 to post on this site

>>62273235
everything’s fake and gay anyway

62273626
>>62273235
Bitcoin is the only crypto club where there’s a long waiting list to get in and you’ll never get turned away because you didn’t follow the dress code that only changed because your ugly ass showed up or even if the club is filled to capacity

62275417
>>62273626
The fuck are you even trying to say?

62275720
>>62272838
enjoy being poor and destitute when the economy is completely overtaken by AI systems and you are relegated to the permanent bottom feeder caste

62276284
>>62275720
LTC WILL NEVER BE CONSIDERED FOR AI YOU INSANE TROON

62278357
>>62276284
sure whatever you say, in 5 years or so your only income stream will come from sucking troon cock anyway

when they'll pay you 0.0002 ltc for each cock sucking session, you'll think back to this thread

62281080
>>62278357
YOU WILL CRY WHEN LTC GOES TO 0.01 LOL LMAO WHAT A CLOWN

62282347
>>62275417
I’m saying you’re a retard with maybe 1/7th knowledge of how bitcoin actually works

62282992
>>62281080
you'll cry after each cock sucking session yourself, regardless of the price of ltc

62283412
LTC CHADS EMERGENCY GATHERING HOW DO WE COPE WITH THE IMMINENT DUMP TO 30$

item.title: IT ALWAYS GOES UP! Bobo is temporary, Mumu is forever! — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
IT ALWAYS GOES UP! Bobo is temporary, Mumu is forever!

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-05-28 11:41 UTC

OP

IT ALWAYS GOES UP!
Bobo is temporary, Mumu is forever!

Early replies (first 3)

62283021
>>62283018
>please buy my overpriced bags before the market crashes

62283022
>please buy my overpriced bags before the market crashes

62283030
>please buy my overpriced bags before the market crashes

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283037
>>62283018
that's a really good point. I just buyed

62283047
now show other market index's like the FSTE 100, is it even above 2008 levels yet????

62283203
>>62283039
ONE nothing wrong with me
TWO

62283258
It's always misleading as fuck
>Your current holdings get rekt by 90%
>It's okay though the stuff you buy from this point onwards is cheap and will grow in recovery
>But what about everything I held before?
>Still rekt for years on end
>We cope by calling that "reducing your average buy in"
Not falling for it personally

62283283
>>62283018
Objective truth. Never doom.

62283382
so why should we be immune from a Japan style 30 year stagnation? We had one before 1960. Allowing retards like Trump in power is begging for it.

62283411
>>62283382
Then buy Chinese or Euro stocks, most of which have already been sideways for ~2 decades.

62283417
>>62283382
Japan largely stopped being innovative desu, not America’s case so far

item.title: Saw this on facebook of all places and it cracked me up - but I'm wondering if anyone has legitimately tried this? — 17 replies — 19.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Saw this on facebook of all places and it cracked me up - but I'm wondering if anyone has legitimately tried this?

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-05-28 10:57 UTC

OP

Saw this on facebook of all places and it cracked me up - but I'm wondering if anyone has legitimately tried this? I can't see it being anywhere near as profitable as OP suggests, nor as easy (what between licencing, food handling certifications and etc.) not to mention how many of you know your average restaurant owner who needs to get their hands on more shrimp? Absolutely no way this would work, right?

Early replies (first 3)

62283250
>>62283243
>facebook
That means some middle aged boomer will now actually do it for the 1,000 followers he still has left.

62283267
>>62283243
1. I believe I've seen this pic before in a shark forum. Like people were raising aquarium sharks in those tanks.

2. Having kept saltwater tanks including very large ones with shrimp, I can burst your /biz/ headcannon bubble pretty easily

Marine shrimps are some of the most expensive and delicate saltwater critters you can keep, and most people simply can't breed them in any commercia quantity because the equipment you use to purify and oxygenate seawater also sucks up and kills shrimps larvae.

commercial shrimp farms get around this by being in the ocean and pumping seawater into their ponds. You cannot do this. So you're going to have a very difficult time keeping shrimps alive as they pass through multiple impellors, filters, UV sterilizers, ozone generators, and protein skimmers.

62283270
>>62283250
I'd still be keen to see that, provided they did a cost evaluation at the end, a boomer would more likely have the money to waste VS a zoomer

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283272
>>62283267
tl;dr: Babby shrimp get suck into filter and die

62283278
>>62283250
>Middle aged boomer
Lmao what a retard

62283281
>>62283243
Everyone stop using Facebook in like 2012, but then I joined the army and all the small town cucks are still posting in this shit. Got out in 2020 and thankfully don't talk to flyovers anymore

62283282
>>62283267
Makes sense. Some of the salt water shrimp need brine water to breed also don't they? Or are the farmer shrimp usually the types that don't need this?

Whats stopping people from farming fresh water shrimp + breeding them to be larger? Although I guess thats just a crayfish huh?

62283290
>>62283282
1. the larger skrimps would need enormous $$$ for salt mix and trace elements to mold, plus equipment and water purification equipment. We're talking hundreds of thousands of dollars. Could be done if you live on the beach, but beachfront property is expensive enought anyone that can afford it is not raising skrimps

B. Freshwater shrimps are almost as demanding about water purity as marine species, and will never get big enough to make a decent cocktail. Though both freshwater and marine skrimps are huge money for the pet trade, as they're worth anything from a few bux for freshwater variants to $40 or $50 EACH for saltwater

62283298
>>62283282
so yeah, people are farming shrimps in their home aquariums. It's not easy and it's certainly not cheap

but they're selling them to pet stores and people who keep them in aquariums. And that's a big money business. Again, not cheap or easy, but you'd get far more cash for live shrimps than you will for food shrimps.

62283307
>>62283243
I love in Florida and we literally scoop shrimp out of the gulf so I'm having a hard time imagine how to make this work

62283308
>>62283290
Got ya, makes sense.

Yeah I can maybe see the value there for selling salt water shrimps as pets but definitely not freshwater. Sure if you get a rare breed and get a solid line going that's cool af, but shrimp breed so easily that unless you're going for those super high quaility (inbred af) lines you'd have to sell quite a few shrimp in a day just to be equalling the money of a normal ass job. Considering how easy say, cherry shrimp breed, I just can't see the demand being high enough to do that for long unless you're selling them directly to customers and not distributors. In which case you're just starting a pet store, which has its own overheads. Hardly a 'get rich quick' scheme and just like... a small fucking business lol.

Rarer / harder to breed shrimp are worth more, but again - because they're rarer and harder to breed / care for. So you're offsetting possible gains with more labor time. Not to mention if one of your tanks suddenly idk explodes you've lost a tonne of value VS a tank filled with cherries.

Have you seen those youtube videos "I tried farming shrimp to get rich" and the like? Always pretty poor returns.

62283314
>>62283307
pretty sure OP's pic is actually shark tanks.

that's a common setup for raising out shark eggs
common dogfish mostly
Possibly epaulettes, bamboos, or coral cats

62283320
>>62283307
I used to live like 5 hours in-land and I think that's the only reason it could be even slightly viable, but I still have doubts. Thats why I wanted to know if anyone had tried it cause like... no way? But also incredibly based if true.

62283324
>>62283308
>Have you seen those youtube videos
I have not, but I did spend close to $150k on saltwater tanks over the years and know the feeling of a tank breaking and losing tens of thousands of dollars in a single shot.

62283334
>>62283324
Dang dude sorry to hear it

62283338
>>62283334
it's part of the game. Anyone that wants a large reef tank has to know they're just flushing cash down the toilet for fun.

and it was fun. I do love keeping fishes.

62283342
>>62283314
those stock tanks would work for growing out benthic instars, but shrimp larvae wouldn't survive that filtration.

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-05-28 09:51 UTC

OP

and their shitcoin is trading at $10

Early replies (first 3)

62282916

62282920
Fuddies are spamming

62282927

HAHAHAHA

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283058
>>62279607
$8 and soon to be worth $3

62283064
>>62283058
Reminder that fuddiesalways overstate their target buyback price, so sell now and buy at $5

62283067
>>62283064
Reminder that cuckolds were calling for triple digits LINK by the end of 2025. So sell now and forget about this shitcoin

62283070
>>62283067
What should they buy with their money instead?

62283081
>>62283070
anything that isn't a fucking premined altcoin from 2017?

62283225
GRIM

item.title: cardano, ADA is imploding right now — 43 replies — 8.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
cardano, ADA is imploding right now

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-05-28 06:58 UTC

OP

cardano, ADA
is imploding right now

Early replies (first 3)

62275698
the whole space is fucking dying. sad that it all ended up this way

62275723
>>62275591
Charles-sama, why?

62276473
>>62275591
Imagine buying cardanus, ever! This shit has less than 500$ stables liquidity onchain.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62277590
>>62277437
Blockchain tech is actually useful in a variety of ways, but it’s not useful to your everyday Joe or Bob. I remember you could play Super Mario 64 on the Internet Computer Protocol (bing bing wahoo on ICP, jej) but who the fuck cares? No one, that’s who.

62277632
>>62277590
Sit down boomer

62277725
>>62275591
What happened?

62278173
>>62277373
Saaar

62278448
>>62277437
>Blockchain tech is actually not that useful

https://www.dtcc.com/news/2026/may/12/dtcc-collaborates-with-chainlink-to-advance-24-7-collateral-management

62278452
What kind of retard do you have to be to be interested in Cardano? Same as polkadot. Jesuschrist, it's a blockchain and they make it out to be like if it was a gothic cathedral.

Hyperliquid is less than 2 years old and is already the most used blockchain.

It was obvious it was all bullshit.

62278457
>>62278452
hyperliquid is closer to a private blockchain. it only has 27 validators.

62278477
>>62276491
This. A major kike stakeholder in a financial scam - never seen before.
In general, I'll never trust a crypto or financial project with a kike or a software project with jeets.

62278491
>>62278477
is Charles a jew? i always assumed he was a goy with all the western shit
>>62277725
i don't know, i sold all my ada in 2021.. but basically charles been scamming the treasury or something for years, makes sense, not surprised.. recently japanese validators or whatever voted to end funding the ""research"" laps funded by cardano.. charles apperently has always gone apeshit and burned every bridge but now it's become exponential bridge burnings and blockings..
ADA is a scam... nothing new.. but finally seeing Charles flame out in real time is kinda neat

62278891
>>62278491

62279915
The cope in this thread is unreal. Cry more, kiddies.

62280492
The narrative haven't really been evolving. Haven't seen your threads for some time and this is the buy signal I honestly was looking and not hoping for. Because the hate your try to project in inconsequential to you.

62280512
I'm not in ADA since 2021.
But now, i am daily scammed by BTC.
Dunno what's worse (financially) desu.

62280533
>>62277725
Some cheap twitter beef causing uproar. It's still one of the most technologically sound chains out there, but some people tried a power grab the minute Charles burnt his keys.
So the only mistake that Charles has made there is that he had too much good faith in those that are stabbing him in the back now. I guess that's what you get if you don't know who your real friends are.

62280595
jeets are still buying crypto lmao
lmfao even

62280622
>>62278448
This cope again
Call me back when stable coins make up less than 99% of all “tokenized real world assets” then maybe I’ll change my mind. Until then I think you’re delusional if you think it’s useful or “mass adoption” to have a handful of turbonerd billionaires and hedge funds use USDC or UST in place of traditional clearinghouses

62280637
>>62277444
But muh Haskell

62280714
>>62280622
>Call me back when stable coins make up less than 99% of all “tokenized real world assets”
DTCC is going to use Chainlink for stablecoins too you know.

62280809
>>62278477
It's a pretty good heuristic

62280833
Save what value you can, swap your ADA for XMR and hodl!

62281020
>>62280595
crypto is banned in india
so in a way, you're like a jeet

62281159
I never trusted Charles so I never bought cardano…dude is an obvious psycho

62281608
ENJOY YOUR CARDANO, EARTHMAN!

62281639
>>62275591
>@TheCardanoTimes

62281653
>>62281159
This, one look at that basedboi faggot should tell you all you need to know

If you still made the decision to put money in this project, its your fault, really

62282581
>>62277151
fucking kek charles is such a fucking narcissist

62282732
>>62277151
Wow that's very bold and stupid. Homeboy thought he could just jump into an industry as complex as healthcare. "Mayo Clinic of the West" lmao

62282767
>>62278448
token not needed

62282801
>>62280714
No they aren't.

62282929
>>62282801

item.title: What do fuddies not understand about $1 per LINK per day? — 50 replies — 6.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
What do fuddies not understand about $1 per LINK per day?

Open thread • Replies: 50 • Updated: 2026-05-28 04:01 UTC

OP

What do fuddies not understand about $1 per LINK per day?

Early replies (first 3)

62274691
>>62274690
Hedera CLPR just ate Chainlink's luch.

62274797
>>62274691
>zero iq take
Theyre on the Hedera council dumbass, which means theyre probably the ones going to implement it.

62274806
>>62274797
>Theyre on the Hedera council dumbass
Irrelevant. CLPR makes CCIP obsolete.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62275388
>>62274690
those banners go for about $300 at fedex office.

62275410
>>62274690
>>62274848
how would an asset worth $9 pay out $1 per day? where does the money come from?

62275479
>>62275410
you are talking to iq80 retards who also yolo'd their meager savings and have no psychological way out besides suicide or delusions.

62275500
>>62275410
Staking returns aren't proportional to the Link price, they're proportional to usage fees which are measured in USD.

62275555
>>62274690
Sounds great when does this start fellow marine?

62275595
>>62274690
>Stinkies really thought some stinky russian made a magical piggie bank that would give them returns beyond their wildest dreams

62275635
>>62275555
Two weeks

Impressive digits btw

62275645
>>62274690
chainlink staking is economic security and the incentive is to pay $1 per link per day? good! KABOOM

62275660
>>62274690
What do you not understand about crypto being dead, Web3 never happening, and Chainlink’s “use case” being obsolete?

62275685
no I meant the price of chainlink will be equivalent to $1for every day of the week
I'm not talking about rewards or anything else
the price of chainlink will be $7

62275738
>>62275660
... and then you woke up and realized DTCC is going into production with Chainlink later this year.

62275779
>>62275385
you said that in another thread, whered you get this info? post sauce

62275789
>>62275738
Oh wow, two years of insiders trading at low volume before people realize tokenized RWA are stupid because why even do that when there’s no chance of outsized returns and doing fraud which were unironically crypto’s biggest selling points?

62275818
>>62275779
>you said that in another thread
I definitely did not, I only researched this myself today.

This is all public information, Hedera, Cosmos, Avalanche have it all laid out in their documentation.

>>62275789
just look at this incoherent seethebabble

62275885
>>62275385
>it's impossible to implement on other chains
This is not what they are saying, just check the hedera sub, it's looking rather grim for ccip

62275919
>>62275818
So no actual counterargument? All you have to do is show me any evidence that DTCC tokenized assets are generating ANY hype right now.

62275933
>>62274690
Ok. But.... price?? Oh.. and chart???

62275992
>>62275933
going up

62276072
>>62275789
lolwut

62276114
>>62275500
>usage fees
What usage?

62276153
>>62274691
>Hedera CLPR just ate Chainlink's luch.
/thread

62276166
>>62275885
>This is not what they are saying
It's not what I'm saying either, you just suck at not being dumb.

>>62276114
DTCC later this year, for instance.

62276216
I'd say chainlink has sizeable state in HBAR. Sergey is not dumb he knew the writing was on he wall when no oher DLT could be as fast or cheap as HBAR.

62277103
>>62275479
ive been found out

62277811
>>62276114
>What usage?
chainlink operates a couple bots that trade between themselves and use their smart contracts so it doesn't look bad. think of it as a marketing expense for them

62277818
>stocks are going parabolic and turd worlders are still dealing in the real world with shitcoins that do nothng
lmao

62278888
A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn $1 per 1 LINK per DAY through staking. Does that mean if I’ve staked 2000 LINK, I’d earn $2000 per day?
Is that true, or was the anon just talking bullshit?

62278920
>>62278888
Imagine how many baby mamas black link marines with 10,000+ link stacks will have.

62282540
>first they ignore you
>then they laugh at you (you are here)
>then they fight you
>then you lose
>then they lock you up

62282577
>>62274963
>>62274690
Ok, so when do we see $1 reward per link staked? Because the dashboard still says 4.32%

Build rewards were a giant nothingburger waste of time and we've seen very few.

item.title: ASTC Astrotech — 4 replies — 6.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
ASTC Astrotech

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-05-28 11:11 UTC

OP

They make spectrometers for drug and explosives detection at airports and events. Boring and solid. Now they announced their intention to mine pure minerals on the moon to run quantum data centers in space. Trading bots let stock skyrocket 600%.

Does such madness out of the blue happen regularly, or is this a top signal?

Early replies (first 3)

62281032
>>62280928
>Does such madness out of the blue happen regularly
yes if you were paying attention

>is this a top signal?
no its algorythic trading to make money and draw in retail for before the price crashes.

62281076
>>62281032
Sorry I don't have much experience, I'm too young.

62283008
>>62280928
>mine pure minerals on the moon to run quantum data centers in space.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283360
>>62280928
>>62283008
kek is this a joke?

item.title: Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now. — 45 replies — 6.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now.

Open thread • Replies: 45 • Updated: 2026-05-28 04:28 UTC

OP

Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now. AMA law related /biz/ questions you may have.

Early replies (first 3)

62272819
>>62272812
Can i peepee in the poopoo or will i go to jail?

62272833
>>62272819
You may

62272835
>>62272812
Is it legal for a president to collab with a news outlet to pump & dump the stock market while insiders make bank on it?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62273171
does it seem like most of your classmates are smart? I was at a high school reunion recently and spoke to a guy who loafed around doing menial work for like 15 years after college. then he took the lsat and got into Northwestern. he has a big time job now defending people in trouble with the SEC.

62273174
>>62273171
Yeah, how many law students are just dumbfucks?

62273234
Junior lawyer is literally technician in legal system. Very far from management and get treated like disposable human trash

62273383
>>62272812
be like this guy anon
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G-6eynsaZ0U&t=836s

62273401
>>62272812
Should I become a lawyer? My college is paid for and I feel like it'll be easy with AI now

62273402
>>62273041
The Irish are better writers the British

62273648
>>62273171
>>62273174
Nobody I'd call dumb but some came across as pretty lazy. Also AI use is absurd.
>>62273401
It's worth it as long as you don't go to a predatory school and don't drown in debt. What really matters is your LSAT and undergrad GPA. You can use this site to put in your undergrad GPA and see what different LSAT scores will get you into and how much in scholarships. It's mostly LSAT though that determines it, but there's a lot of free LSAT test prep stuff online and it is a very learnable test.
https://lsd.law/law-school-chances-predictor

62273654
Lawyers will be replaced with A.I.

62275142
>>62273654
Sort of like how your dad was replaced by my penis

62275176
>>62272812
Tits or ass

62275435
>>62272812

Are you going to be an actual trial attorney with balls or be a paper pushing cuck like most?

I can't make up my mind who's intelligence the general public overrates more between doctors and lawyers.

62275484
>>62272812
What was your LSAT score? What is your school’s rank? What was your GPA? Where are you interning this summer?

How big is your pp?

62275816
>>62275176
Ass for most of my life but as I’ve gotten older, tits
>>62275435
I honestly don’t know yet, it’s only been one year and I’m not even dead set on which area of the law I want to practice in.
>>62275484
>What was your LSAT score?
170
>What is your school’s rank?
Mid 20s
>What was your GPA?
In undergrad or 1L? In undergrad it was a 3.4 in an easy major, I just didn’t give a shit in college. In 1L it was a 3.5
>Where are you interning this summer?
I’m RAing with a professor and doing a one month internship with a local judge later in the summer.

>How big is your pp?
5.8 inches massive

62275842
Do you ever consider yourself a second class profession compared to finance chads who value and trade and make money off the silly little financial obligation contracts lawyers write up?

62275952
>>62275842
Again dude I just finished my first out of three years of law school, so I don’t know what the actual practice looks like at all.

62276043
>>62275952
I'm just ribbing you buddy, a little white collar banter if you will

62277022
>>62275816
>170 LSAT

Acceptable.

>Mid 20s Law School

Acceptable.

>3.5 1L GPA

Acceptable.

>I’m RAing with a professor and doing a one month internship with a local judge later in the summer.

It’s over. You are cooked. BIG oof. Thanks for playing.

62277039
>>62277022
Why is it over? And why didn’t you address my penis size?

62277080
>>62277039
If you are not interning at an AMLAW 50 firm your 1L summer then you literally need to kill yourself.

[spoiler]My posts are intended to satirize the rampant one-upsmanship in the legal profession. I don’t actually care about your genitalia you dumb retard. It is a metaphor.[/spoiler]

62278084
>>62277080
Well don’t scare me like that anon. You freaked me out since some of the people in my class do have Big Law internships and it’s kinda tough not to feel jealous sometimes with how much they’re getting paid.

62278202
>>62278084
Funposting aside, (You) should be (mildly) freaked out because spending your 1L summer split between volunteering for a professor and a one (1) month stint with a (non-federal) jjudge is concerning. Especially if the stats (You) quoted me earlier are true.

(You) are underachieving.

62278255
>>62278202
I don't know what to tell you. I applied for a billion 1L summer positions and even got a lot of interviews but none of them panned out. It would have been nice to get something more prestigious looking but I'm just glad I finally got something lined up. i have heard also that as long as you do do something legal related 1L summer then firms don't really care what it was, is that not true?

I'm not actually aiming for Big Law or anything, I'm mostly interested in environmental and real estate law and potentially being a prosecutor. I dunno I've only taken the basic classes and it feels weird this expectation to know for certain what you want to do before you've learned anything about the field.

62278315
>>62278255
I appreciate your sincerity, and I will stop frogposting.

Forget about prestige. Focus on what will get you paid. I encourage you to network with local boutique and midsize firms. These days, Big Law internships are reserved for black lesbians (unironically). You’re doing fine.

Godspeed.

62278366
>>62278315
>Forget about prestige. Focus on what will get you paid
I am getting paid for the RA job though.
>I encourage you to network with local boutique and midsize firms
This might be self-evident as a 4chan poster but I absolutely hate networking, really suck at it, and would like to avoid it as much as possible. Is it really that essential to getting a good job? Do I just have to force myself to do it? I heard the 2L summer hiring process was supposed to be way chiller and less insane.

And thanks for your advice by the way!

62278395
>>62278366
Networking is somewhat required for any career path unfortunately. My condolences anon

62280144
>>62278395

62280365
>>62278366
You will miss out of some of the best opportunities in this profession if you don’t network, unfortunately. I only got my current job because one of my professors was willing to refer me to someone on the inside.

62280758
Are there any good legal loopholes for evading debt collection? I'm writing a story, and there's a chapter where the MC scrambles to hide his assets from the chapter 7 trustee.

62281712
>>62280758
I know several but will tell you none

62282639
>>62280758
>I'm writing a story
I dunno if I believe you there anon

item.title: lever up on XLM now. — 4 replies — 5.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
lever up on XLM now.

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-05-28 11:07 UTC

OP

this pile of shit is always good for the yearly crime candle and it's due with the DTCC news.
>Only coin in the top 100 green today
>Massive spike in volume on spot and perps (2nd day)
They'll crime this one for a at least a few more days. Time to make your money back anon. Ogs remember the 2021 xlm crime candle that kickstarted the bull cycle.

Early replies (first 3)

62283130
buncha noobs also going to short it cus muh mean reversion everything else is crashing!

62283353
>>62283344

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283356
>>62283117
What is a crime candle?

item.title: SpaceX has a total addressable market almost the size of the US GDP. — 32 replies — 5.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
SpaceX has a total addressable market almost the size of the US GDP.

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-05-28 05:38 UTC

OP

SpaceX has a total addressable market almost the size of the US GDP. Why aren't you investing in their IPO?

Early replies (first 3)

62273501
>>62273487
If AI ends up being a nothing burger, then SpaceX is little more than a flashy telecommunications company.
Everyone in the world is betting the farm on AI too, what happens if a different company achieves AGI before SpaceX?

62273502
People still don't understand what printing money does to an economy.

62273506
>>62273487
>SpaceX
>Space
the name of the fucking company is 0.1% of its TAM golly gee maybe they should call it AIX?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62273511
>>62273487
Space is bigger than America so the profits are infinite
Elon really knows how to win

62273537
I'll probably give em a hundred bucks, see how it goes.

62273604
Everyone holding a broad index fund, which is pretty much every American with a retirement account, will be inadvertently investing in this IPO. We will all be Elong Muk's exit liquidity. I can't wait to support a company that loses $5 billion/year.

62273611
>>62273604
Can't you just sell your index fund and then buy it later once the stock has dropped 90%?

62273615
IDK about space, but I'm bullish on tesla and U.S. stocks. I still think rockets are a scam and they already have UFO's that are capable of long distance space travel.

By 2030 solid state battery electric cars will be here in full force in tesla for consumers, making ev's and they'll be exceptional deals, 300K miles with barely any wear compared to a gas engine car. Incredibly efficient compared to gas cars and last longer.

Agentic AI will be in cars, self driving will be good by then.

62273662
>>62273487
"SpaceX estimates it's own TAM by making up a bunch of bullshit"

62273749
>>62273511
Its not, according to muricans.

62274456
>>62273749
Well, it’s not any bigger than Texas.
That’s why they have to launch the rockets from Texas.

62274498
>>62273487
SpaceX TAM eternally va US GDP In one year.

62274836
>>62274498
TAM is measured in annual revenue

62275036
>>62273487
Maybe if he was mining asteroids for precious metals, but this is ridiculous
Twitter is not worth that much money
Grok isn't worth that much money
This is a scam

62275067
>>62275036
Asteroid mining is the kind of thing that sounds like a good idea, until you think about the practicalities of running mining equipment and refining plants in microgravity, the delta-v requirements of bringing the stuff home, and the likely lack of any easily accessible ices that could be turned into rocket propellant on any asteroid that orbits anywhere near the sun

62275088
>Why aren't you investing in their IPO?
There aren't enough shares for plebs like me to short.
Same reason I sat in the cuck chair as people in my discord made six figures on CBRS shorts. No volume for plebbies.

62275111
I am going to put $500 into SpaceX and then hopefully one day long after I am dead my money will make it to the moons of Saturn

62275148
>>62275111
It will moke likely crash into Uranus after the IPO

62275195
>>62273487
>IPO
Point me to an IPO that wasn't a bag-holder hand-off from private equity and didn't immediately lose large chunks of its value after the first day.

62275335
>>62273611
The problem isn't losing money; it's tens of millions if not more basically being forced to have a stake in what's going to be a shit show of an IPO. Most people won't even know that this is happening.

There are also a lot of people with target date retirement funds and reallocating their portfolio, while doable, isn't something that they should have to worry about because Nasdaq changed the rules to allow mega-caps to enter the Nasdaq-100 after 15 days following the IPO. The same thing is going to happen with Scam Altman-Bankman-Fried's OpenAI when that goes public.

62276783
>>62273615
By definition these would not be UFOs then since UFO stands for unidentified flight object.

62276797
>>62273487
It's a scam. I read it's IPO. Unaudited financials. Absolute disaster. There are dog coins with better fundementals

62276808
>>62275335
Pension funds won't even touch software at the moment let alone this shitshow. reddit has this deep need to give Elon their money no matter what but as a stock it is hot garbage.

62276827
>>62275195
Mixue (2097.HK) had a three-month long +100% bull run just after its IPO last year, but now it’s down of course

I don’t think Google/Alphabet ever went back to its IPO level once

62277088
>>62276797
>Unaudited financials.
[citation needed]

62277349
>>62276808
Nigga Reddit hates musky

62277355
>>62275195
Facebook

62277449
>>62273501
AGI will literally never be achieved lmao

62278547
>>62273501
Its gonna be a nothing burger then the government says "whoops we need to bail you out, seize the data centers, oh how convienient you have exactly the hardware we need to run a surveillance state that puts china to shame"

62278606
>>62273487
What's the upside when the IPO is at the peak valuation?

62280676
>>62273615
I love EVs, but boy do they need to get their rusting problems under control.

62282746
You know it's a bubble when a rocket company estimates that their rocket operations is only 1% of the value of their business

item.title: AAAAAAAAAAAAHHH — 15 replies — 4.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
AAAAAAAAAAAAHHH

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-05-28 08:30 UTC

OP

I know one of you is or knows someone influential so please shill LINK so my REAL life can start.
Joe I know you browse here. Get Sergey on the podcast to make up for getting h*m into office.

Early replies (first 3)

62275233
>>62274897
someone has to hold the bag anon. It's your turn. thanks for existing. 0 sum game

62275347
>>62274897
anon you have to accept that you were scammed.

you are a lickcuck stick in hell. sell your bags and start over.

62277824
wasn't the price in the double digits? that's rough lol

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62277832
it's the #16 ranked coin in all of crypto... what more do you want lol.

62277838
>>62274897
Its up like 20% from the bottom

62277863
Never.

62278450
1.... nothing wrong with me
2.... nothing wrong with me

62278460
>>62274897
guess what happened here

62278696
>>62278460
Sergey posted a Tweet mocking Link cucks?

62278712
>>62278460
i sold

62281342
>>62278460
you bought?

62282658
>>62278460
pain and misery for all stinkies :}

62282692
>>62277863
>that pic

Just lmao

62282819
>>62277838
This. We are doing great and fudders keep crying

62283072
>>62282819
>we are doing great
the absolute state of cuck marines lol

item.title: Jannies, excuse me while I KILL MYSELF — 22 replies — 3.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Jannies, excuse me while I KILL MYSELF

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-05-28 05:44 UTC

OP

It's fucked for real this time. completely dead. AI bubble will pop, S&P will collapse and drag BTC even lower, at a certain point Saylor will get liquidated. Tom lee will get liquidated. BTC at $15k and ETH at $200 is not unrealistic. Short all crypto. I've been in this space for a decade. It's truly dead. Saylor will be sucking cock in hell wondering where it all went wrong. Tom Lee will shove a pineapple up his own ass. We are FUCKED. IT IS FUCKING OVER. CRYPTO IS DEAD. THE CLARITY ACT WILL NEVER PASS. BLACKROCK WILL DIVEST. BINANCE WILL SHUT DOWN. WE ARE ALL FUCKED AND GOING TO SUCK COCK IN HELL. VITALIK WILL BE PERFORMING FELATIO ON ORTHODOX JEWISH RABBI IN HELL. WE BOUGHT A FUCKING PET ROCK FAKE PIECE OF FAGGOT SHIT ABANDON SHIP NOW NOW !!!!!!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62281675
whoopsie poopsie xD

62281676
????????????

62281679
Better luck next time

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62281685
>>62281679
Oh my FUCKING GOD

62281691
JUST IN TIME FOR CHAINLINKS 7 YEAR ANIVERSARY ON MAINNET AND THE RELEASE OF INEFERNO TO BOOT LMAOOO

62281698
>>62281668
Should've bought HBAR...!

62281705
>pink ID

Yeah you’re fucked buddy

62281711
Literal bottom signal

62281713
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6bghDcbzfEU

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=74NluS3jzTo

brb meds

62281742
OP here. I'm back. I'm more relaxed and thinking logically now. Realistically they are going to begin having military style tribunals for crypto advocates. Arthur hayes will be buck broken on CSPAN. Tom lee will shove a pineapple up his own ass. Satoshi nakatoto will be finally revealed and be drawn.and quartered on national television (in israel for some reason) we are so fucked. It is so over. (Not reading replies)

62281754
>>62281668
ive been in crypto since 2016

you are an emotional pussy nothing will happen or ever happen stfu

62281776
>>62281754
Fuck you homo. Tom Lee pineapple prepping faggot

62281782
>>62281776
you said you weren't reading replies!

62281789
>>62281668
>ai bubble pops
>new tech fad emerges
>tech giants become even more profitable than before
>spx goes up nonstop forever
>crypto remains a pump and dump scamcoin forever

62281798
>>62281776
Your father is a FAGGOT and your mother FUCKS NIGGERS

62281831
>>62281798
Alright this is true

62281862
>>62281742
no crying in the nigger casino

62281983
>>62281776
no fuck you nigger god u guys just cry nonstop fuck u fuck u

62282258
We're all gonna burn in brimstone niggerhell for our investments

62282479
It’s over.

62282734
>>62281862
Bitcoin people are deluded. Some retarded fuck said that btc would be the reserve currency of the world. Can you imagine if that actually happened? Imagine a huge crisis happened, and you needed to dilute the chaos by issuing some money to the struggling businesses. What are you gonna do lmao? forcibly divide everyone's bitcoins up and move the price up? every lost bitcoin is a lost unit of "reserve currency" btw

62282760
>Jannies, excuse me while I

DONT forget to livestream it faggot
